---
title: "Spotify Wrapped"
subtitle: "The Flavour"
author: "Matteo Torres"
date: "January 01, 2025"
execute: 
  output: false
format: 
  html:
    theme: darkly
    toc: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## Load Packages
```{r}
#| code-fold: true

library(tidyverse)
library(googlesheets4)
```

## Read Data
```{r}
# Create a reproducible file path
url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1U2XrypJcbxnK-hb4GnnqcOejY00HU1kcCVIfWHB_Q7A/edit?usp=sharing"
spotify_data <- read_sheet(url)
```

## Data Exploration
```{r, output = TRUE}
# Look at the first six rows of the dataset
head(spotify_data)
```

## Data Cleaning
```{r, output = TRUE}
# Save the current column names
current_colnames <- colnames(spotify_data)

# Add the current column names as the first row
spotify_data <- rbind(current_colnames, spotify_data)

# Assign new column names
new_colnames <- c("date", "track_name", "artist", "track_id", "link")
colnames(spotify_data) <- new_colnames

# Verify update
head(spotify_data)
```

```{r, output = TRUE}
# Remove unnecessary characters from the date
spotify_data$date <- gsub(" at", "", spotify_data$date)

# Update the data type for the date column
spotify_data$date <- mdy_hm(spotify_data$date)

# Verify update
head(spotify_data)
```

## Data Analysis

*insert text about top 5 artists...*
```{r, output = TRUE}
#| code-fold: true

# Count streams per artist
n_streams <- spotify_data %>%
  group_by(artist) %>%
  summarize(total_streams = sum(n())) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_streams))

# Save the top five artists
top_5_artists <- head(n_streams, n = 5)

# Create a barplot
ggplot(data = top_5_artists, 
       aes(x = reorder(artist, -total_streams), 
           y = total_streams)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", 
           fill = "#8ACE00") +
  labs(x = "Artist",
       y = "Total Number of Streams (n)",
       title = "Top 5 Artists of the Month!") + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, family = "Courier"),  
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Courier"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Courier"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Courier"),
    plot.title = element_text(family = "Courier", size = 14))
```

*insert text about top 5 songs...*

```{r, output = TRUE}
#| code-fold: true

# Count the top tracks
top_tracks <- spotify_data %>%
  group_by(track_name) %>%
  summarize(total_streams = sum(n())) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_streams))

# Save the top five tracks
top_5_tracks <- head(top_tracks, n = 5)

# Create a barplot
ggplot(data = top_5_tracks, 
       aes(x = reorder(track_name, -total_streams), 
           y = total_streams)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", 
           fill = "#8ACE00") +
  labs(x = "Track Title",
       y = "Total Number of Streams (n)",
       title = "Top 5 Songs of the Month!") + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, family = "Courier"),  
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Courier"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Courier"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Courier"),
    plot.title = element_text(family = "Courier", size = 14))
```

## Data Citations
